# Transcrição: 03--Reduce CPM via The ViPER Test for Better Page Engagement

## [00:00 - 03:00]

This is what I call the page viper test. Viper is viral page engagement reach test. And what I'm trying to do here is to run a split test within Facebook to find out the content that's gonna resonate for my page. This works for e-commerce, it works for info, works for brand, celebrity pages, whatever it is, to find the content that's gonna resonate. So it's a real super powerful method of finding content that you can use for your kind of top of funnel and your existing customer campaigns. It can also feed into your email marketing, et cetera. So it's a real cool test to find the content that's really gonna make a difference to your campaigns as well. Now I like to run it as a Facebook split test because I can fairly test it without any worry about, you know, if they've seen this ad, maybe they've seen the second ad as well. The Facebook split test tool allows you to just split that traffic however many ways you want it to make sure that people only ever see that one variant, for example. Now when I run my tests, I will either choose a page post that's already on my page, or I will create a new post and just run it as what you'd call a dark post or a hidden post, which is just an ad. And then I'd run that as my split test and whichever ad performs best, I would then publish that on my page. So this is the kind of overview of the test process. So the goal is really to identify the most engaging content through our Viper test. So first of all, I would create a split test campaign in Facebook and you can see the example layout on the right hand side. I would use the page post engagement objective. The audience I go for is all Facebook fans for my page, and I only test this on the Facebook news feed only. Because what I want to do is to find content that's gonna be, that I can drop on my Facebook page. Therefore it makes sense to just test it on the news feed only. Then in terms of variables for my split test, I want at least two creatives to test as you can't split test unless you have more than two. Generally I end up with three or four in this example here on the right, you can see three. The budget overall for the campaign is a minimum of $10 a day. I would generally run it at $25 to $50, but it depends on how big your page is as well. Then in terms of your posts, you can either create your post via the ads library. So if you've been through graduation testing, you'll know what the ads library is. So you'd create your original post there and share the post ID across into your split test. then you can select them here as well. Run that to conclusion, which means that Facebook is giving you a kind of result of kind of which one performed best. Now, if you just literally just drop it onto your page and you've got 10,000 fans, you're gonna struggle to reach more than maybe 250, 500 people just based on how organic reach works on your page. And then the reason I do my ad testing is because I want to reach everyone or as many people as I can in that audience. So if I have a page with 10,000 people that are fans, then I want to put some budget behind them, behind reaching as many people as I can to get that validated feedback. I then take that kind of signal for whichever posts I'm resonating to now give me ideas, not only on what I should post on the page to increase my engagement, but also what to push in my ad. But the positive signals here, when you have a page and you have a post getting high engagement from those fans, sends a real positive signal back to Facebook that says, this page is legit. And what I mean by that is, if you are in the space of a kind of fast money business, let's say for example, you're doing affiliate marketing or dropship or anything like that, where you're running ads and you want to scale it fast and get your profits out, but you don't really pay attention to your page. Facebook recognize this. So when you're actually developing your page, you're engaging people on your page, your fans are engaging with your post, it's a strong signal to Facebook that you're a legit business, you care about your users, and you want to generate more of a kind of brand experience. So if you look at the kind of bigger brands that have hundreds of thousands, if not millions of fans, they really are pushing out content to try and engage them. There are brands that do this really poorly, where they will just literally post blog posts, blog posts, blog posts, et cetera. When you are posting to your page, if you take someone away from Facebook, you're not going to get the benefit of engagement.

## [06:00 - 06:57]

So for example, if you are posting blog posts to your page, that's fine, but vary it with content that engages. So for example, if it's depending on what your brand and kind of brand message is, if there's a kind of engaging or funny video that people are just playing and commenting, but staying in the newsfeed, that will have a more positive impact than, for example, just putting, like I've seen many Econ brands, that all they do is they post their new products and new releases, and then they're taking that traffic from the page off into their site. That doesn't have as positive an impact as just sharing content that engages them and keeps them on Facebook. So there's something to balance out, but really the Viper test is all about seeing which of those posts and which of the content will resonate with your fans to give you an idea of what content to push into your page. And then you can use that to pull out into your Top of Funnel ad campaign as well.
